name: Eastern Bus Co
districts_or_customers: Boston/Cambridge-area school districts, museums, colleges, churches, arts groups; serves ~11 communities in greater Boston
recent_news: FMCSA snapshot (2024) shows 251 power units / 250 drivers and 3.25M annual miles; active non-ratable carrier; 2018 MA SJC case Casseus v. Eastern Bus.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: independent, founder-led (Winitzer founded 1997), ~250 buses with Boston-area school district and charter book; mid-size scale and aging founder suggest succession opportunity. Note input file's HQ/website fields (Newton, Flixbus.com) are incorrect; real HQ is 1 Durham St, Somerville MA.

name: Community Bus Services Inc
principals: Kathy Laughlin (HR Executive listed); ownership not publicly disclosed
districts_or_customers: School districts and human service agencies across 9 Ohio counties: Ashtabula, Cuyahoga, Franklin, Geauga, Lake, Licking, Mahoning, Montgomery, Trumbull; includes Groveport Madison district; ~4M student trips/yr, ~100K special-needs trips/yr, ~3M annual miles
recent_news: Long-established (since 1933) Ohio statewide contractor providing GE and SPED transportation plus management/maintenance services to districts; no public M&A activity found.
fit_notes: High-quality tuck-in: ~90-year-old Ohio statewide contractor with multi-county school+SPED footprint and management/maintenance services. Independent and not visibly PE-owned. Scale (4M trips/3M miles) suggests several hundred buses — meaningful platform addition for Summit in OH.

name: Roberts Hawaii
owner_or_parent: Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) / Iwamoto family minority
principals: Robert Iwamoto Jr. (Chairman); Roy Pfund (President/CEO succession)
districts_or_customers: Hawaii State Department of Education (won 181 of 305 Oahu routes in 2013, $21M contract); statewide presence across Kauai, Oahu, Maui, Hawaii Island; tours, shuttles, hotels, entertainment
recent_news: Transitioned to ESOP in 2008 (Iwamoto family transferred 47% to employees); dominates HI school bus contracting historically; opened new Limelight Hawaii entertainment venue in March 2026.
fit_notes: Poor tuck-in fit: ESOP-owned diversified tourism/transport conglomerate (~900 vehicles, 1,400 employees, hotels/cruises/entertainment); school bus is only one division of a much larger HI-only business. Not an independent family operator and complex to carve out; geography (Hawaii) also outside Summit's contiguous-US footprint.

name: Hicksville Bus Lot LLC
principals: Muriel Dalton (registered agent/owner per NY filings)
districts_or_customers: Spring Valley/Rockland County area; likely East Ramapo CSD ecosystem (yeshiva and/or fleet contractor) — not directly confirmed
recent_news: NY LLC formed Oct 9, 2015 at 56 W Church St, Spring Valley NY; related entity 'Bus Lot Management LLC' at same address formed 2008. No operating website, no carrier news found.
fit_notes: Low-confidence target: name 'Bus Lot' plus sister entity 'Bus Lot Management' at same address suggests this may be a real-estate/parking holding LLC rather than an operating school-bus contractor. Employee count (110) is from third-party data and unverified. Diligence required to confirm whether it is an operator vs. a yard/holding entity tied to East Ramapo yeshiva transport.

name: Smart Pick Bus Inc
principals: Seth Benowitz (legacy manager); Joseph Sgro (First Student regional contact)
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE school bus routes (Brooklyn, NYC) — operating under First Student umbrella
recent_news: Smart Pick has been folded into First Student's NYC operations: First Student posts Smart Pick driver/monitor jobs from firststudentinc.com and the contact email is joseph.sgro@firststudentinc.com, indicating Smart Pick is now part of First Student's Brooklyn network (EQT Infrastructure portfolio). FMCSA shows 60 power units/61 drivers.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target: Smart Pick is operating under First Student (EQT-owned), Summit's largest competitor. Exclude from independent-operator universe.
